Example: Bootstrap Confidence Intervals (bs.*)

Source: vignettes/bs-bootstrap-example.qmd 
library(TemporalHazard)
library(hvtiPlotR)

These examples correspond to the bs.* SAS files: bootstrap confidence intervals for hazard function estimates and model summaries. Bootstrap CI is the primary uncertainty quantification method used in the HAZARD workflow.

bs.death.AVC — Bootstrap CI for AVC death hazard

SAS source: examples/bs.death.AVC.sas

Purpose: Generate bootstrap confidence intervals for the parametric hazard function fitted to AVC death data. Each bootstrap replicate refits the model and accumulates prediction curves; the CI is then summarized across replicates.

SAS (original) 

%BOOTSTRAP(
  DATA    = AVCS,
  HAZEST  = EXAMPLES.HMDEATH,
  N       = 200,
  SEED    = 12345,
  OUT     = BSOUT
);

R translation (runnable M3 example) 

set.seed(3201)
n <- 280
avcs <- data.frame(
  INT_DEAD = rexp(n, rate = 0.6),
  AGE = rnorm(n, 6, 3),
  COM_IV = rbinom(n, 1, 0.2),
  MAL = rbinom(n, 1, 0.2),
  OPMOS = runif(n, 0, 18),
  OP_AGE = runif(n, 0, 12),
  STATUS = sample(1:4, n, replace = TRUE),
  INC_SURG = rbinom(n, 1, 0.12),
  ORIFICE = rbinom(n, 1, 0.1)
)

lp <- -2.5 + 0.05 * avcs$AGE + 0.6 * avcs$COM_IV + 0.4 * avcs$MAL +
  0.25 * (avcs$STATUS >= 3) + 0.4 * avcs$INC_SURG
avcs$DEAD <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(lp))

X <- data.matrix(avcs[, c("AGE", "COM_IV", "MAL", "OPMOS", "OP_AGE",
                          "STATUS", "INC_SURG", "ORIFICE")])

fit_hm <- hazard(
  time = avcs$INT_DEAD,
  status = avcs$DEAD,
  x = X,
  theta = c(mu = 0.25, nu = 1.10, rep(0, ncol(X))),
  dist = "weibull",
  fit = TRUE,
  control = list(maxit = 400)
)

time_grid <- data.frame(time = seq(0.1, 4.0, length.out = 40))

# Bootstrap loop (smaller n for vignette runtime).
set.seed(12345)
n_boot <- 60

boot_curves <- replicate(n_boot, simplify = FALSE, {
  idx    <- sample(nrow(avcs), replace = TRUE)
  boot_d <- avcs[idx, ]
  boot_x <- data.matrix(boot_d[, c("AGE", "COM_IV", "MAL", "OPMOS", "OP_AGE",
                                   "STATUS", "INC_SURG", "ORIFICE")])

  out <- tryCatch({
    boot_fit <- hazard(
      time   = boot_d$INT_DEAD,
      status = boot_d$DEAD,
      x      = boot_x,
      theta  = coef(fit_hm),
      dist   = "weibull",
      fit    = TRUE,
      control = list(maxit = 250)
    )

    predict(boot_fit, newdata = cbind(time_grid, as.data.frame(boot_x[1:nrow(time_grid), ])),
            type = "survival")
  }, error = function(e) {
    rep(NA_real_, nrow(time_grid))
  })

  out
})

length(boot_curves)

bs.death.AVC.summary — Bootstrap summary statistics

SAS source: examples/bs.death.AVC.summary.sas

Purpose: Compute percentile-based confidence bands from bootstrap replicates and produce publication-ready hazard function plots with shaded CI.

R translation (runnable M3 example) 

ci_bands <- do.call(rbind, boot_curves)
ci_bands <- ci_bands[rowSums(is.na(ci_bands)) == 0, , drop = FALSE]
median_surv <- apply(ci_bands, 2, median)
lower <- apply(ci_bands, 2, quantile, probs = 0.025)
upper <- apply(ci_bands, 2, quantile, probs = 0.975)

bootstrap_summary <- data.frame(
  time = time_grid$time,
  surv_median = median_surv,
  surv_lower = lower,
  surv_upper = upper
)

head(bootstrap_summary)

# Plot parametric survival with bootstrap 95% CI
hz_bs <- hv_hazard(
  curve_data   = data.frame(
    time     = bootstrap_summary$time,
    survival = bootstrap_summary$surv_median * 100,
    lower    = bootstrap_summary$surv_lower * 100,
    upper    = bootstrap_summary$surv_upper * 100
  ),
  x_col        = "time",
  estimate_col = "survival",
  lower_col    = "lower",
  upper_col    = "upper"
)

plot(hz_bs) +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Years after repair",
    y = "Freedom from death (%)",
    title = "Bootstrap 95% CI — AVC death hazard model"
  ) +
  hv_theme()
